rationale: The cited paper could be a general behavioral-finance background source (earmarking/partitioning effects on savings), which is at least thematically related to the citing paper’s focus on psychological effects in financial choices beyond NPV. However, the available match evidence is only a DOI entry (no in-text citation context, title string, or quoted surrounding text), and the two studies differ substantially in setting and method (rural India field savings intervention vs U.S. annuity conjoint), so it is not safe to classify as obvious mention-only.
